checks.a_analysis: I have compared each text element in the JSON output with the source PDF. All paragraphs and sentences in the output correspond to text present in the original document. No invented headings or body text were found. The image alt text is descriptive of the seal present in the document and is a permitted AI-generated addition.
The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, mailing addresses, or URLs. Consequently, the output contains none of these elements, and no fabrication has occurred.

The source document consists of a single page containing a header, a title, several 'WHEREAS' clauses, a resolution statement, a date, signature blocks, and an official seal. All of these components are represented in the JSON output in the correct sequence.
The source document contains no tables. This check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A thorough word-for-word comparison shows that no significant text has been omitted from any section. The output captures the full text of the resolution clauses and the associated official names and titles.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the JSON output maintains the exact meaning and wording of the source PDF. Minor reformatting like line breaks in the header or the handling of the superscript in the date are considered acceptable. There are no changes to numbers, dates, or legal language that would alter the information provided to a reader.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains each section of the document exactly once, mirroring the source PDF. There are no repeated paragraphs, signature blocks, or clauses.

The output uses an H2 for the main document title. While H1 is often preferred for titles, the use of H2 is consistent and logical within the structure provided, and there are no level skips or incorrect nesting.
The source document does not contain any tables, and the output correctly reflects this with a table count of zero.

The reading order in the JSON follows the source PDF exactly from top to bottom: header information, title, preamble clauses (WHEREAS), resolution clause, date, and final signature blocks. The image of the seal is placed at the end, which is logical for a linear data structure.

The source document does not utilize bulleted or numbered lists. The sequence of 'WHEREAS' clauses are correctly rendered as individual paragraphs, preserving their semantic function without forcing them into a list structure.
The source document contains no hyperlinks or URLs. The output correctly contains no links.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is faithfully preserved in the HTML conversion. The hierarchy of information—heading, jurisdiction, resolution clauses, and signatory blocks—follows the same logical flow as the source PDF. The title has been placed at the top of the document, which is a standard and expected layout adjustment for accessible HTML. The transition from the PDF's fixed layout to the HTML's single-column, responsive layout is clean and does not confuse the meaning of the content.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable and passes.

The source PDF contains a prominent, meaningful logo: the official gold seal of Rockingham County located at the bottom right of the page. This seal is a meaningful graphical element that denotes the official nature of the resolution. The rendered HTML output fails to include this image. While the handwritten signatures are also missing, their meaning is captured by the transcribed text names; however, the seal (a logo) is an explicit requirement of this check and its omission constitutes a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF shows a circular gold seal for "ROCKINGHAM COUNTY NORTH CAROLINA" at the bottom right of the document.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML rendering contains no image element or graphical representation of the Rockingham County seal.
checks.b_evidence_location: Bottom right corner of the source document.

checks.a_analysis: There is no duplication of body content in the HTML output. Each 'WHEREAS' clause, the 'NOW, THEREFORE' section, and the signature blocks appear exactly once. While the document title appears at the very top as a main heading, this is standard accessible design and does not count as content duplication within the body.
